Metadata.Coram: Chao Hick Tin JA; L P Thean JA; Yong Pung How CJ
Metadata.Judge: Chao Hick Tin JA; L P Thean JA; Yong Pung How CJ

Party_Details.Name: Kea Holdings Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Gan Boon Hock joined Kea Resources as a general manager.
Gan Boon Hock served as managing director of Kea Resources until this date.
Gan became a majority shareholder and director of Sinindo Pacific Pte Ltd.
Kea Resources forfeited deposits totaling US$77,610 due to cancellation of barge orders.
Gan allegedly procured two sale agreements for the vessel 'Regal 8' with different sale prices.
Appellants commenced proceedings against Gan for management obligations and fraudulent misrepresentation.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a director can be held legally responsible for diverting business opportunities and misrepresenting financial transactions to the detriment of the company.
Party_Details.Rule: A director must act in the best interests of the company and avoid conflicts between personal interests and company duties.
Party_Details.Application: The appellants stated that Gan diverted business and misrepresented sale agreements, causing financial loss. Gan argued he acted in the best interests of Sinindo and denied any misrepresentation.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found Gan liable for misrepresentation in the sale of 'Orient VI' and ordered compensation for forfeited deposits, but dismissed claims related to business diversion and 'Regal 8'.

Metadata.Coram: Chao Hick Tin JA; L P Thean JA; Yong Pung How CJ
Metadata.Judge: Chao Hick Tin JA; L P Thean JA; Yong Pung How CJ

Party_Details.Name: Gan Boon Hock

Facts.Text: Gan Boon Hock joined Kea Resources as a general manager.
Gan Boon Hock served as managing director of Kea Resources until this date.
Gan became a majority shareholder and director of Sinindo Pacific Pte Ltd.
Gan informed appellants of his involvement in Sinindo, claiming it was to lend his name to Teddy Salim.
Kea Resources forfeited deposits totaling US$77,610 due to cancellation of barge orders.
Gan allegedly procured two sale agreements for the vessel 'Regal 8' with different sale prices.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a director is legally responsible for stated misrepresentation and diversion of business opportunities.
Party_Details.Rule: A director must act in the best interests of the company and avoid conflicts between personal interests and company duties.
Party_Details.Application: Gan argued he acted in the best interests of Sinindo and denied any misrepresentation. The appellants stated that Gan diverted business and misrepresented sale agreements, causing financial loss.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found Gan liable for misrepresentation in the sale of 'Orient VI' and ordered compensation for forfeited deposits, but dismissed claims related to business diversion and 'Regal 8'.

Metadata.Coram: Chao Hick Tin JA; L P Thean JA; Yong Pung How CJ
Metadata.Judge: Chao Hick Tin JA; L P Thean JA; Yong Pung How CJ

Party_Details.Name: Gan Boon Hock

Facts.Text: Gan claimed annual wage supplements and bonuses from Kea Resources.
Gan joined Kea Resources as a general manager.
Gan served as managing director of Kea Resources until this date.
Gan counterclaimed against Kea Resources for financial entitlements.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether an employee is entitled to claim unpaid financial entitlements after termination of employment.
Party_Details.Rule: An employee may claim financial entitlements if they are contractually agreed upon and remain unpaid.
Party_Details.Application: Gan stated that he was owed financial entitlements as per his employment contract. Kea Resources contested the validity of these claims.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court allowed Gan's counterclaim for financial entitlements, awarding him S$42,166.67 and CPF contributions.

Metadata.Coram: Chao Hick Tin JA; L P Thean JA; Yong Pung How CJ
Metadata.Judge: Chao Hick Tin JA; L P Thean JA; Yong Pung How CJ

Party_Details.Name: Kea Resources Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Gan joined Kea Resources as a general manager.
Gan served as managing director of Kea Resources until this date.
Gan claimed annual wage supplements and bonuses from Kea Resources.
Gan counterclaimed against Kea Resources for financial entitlements.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a company is legally responsible to pay financial entitlements claimed by a former employee.
Party_Details.Rule: A company is obligated to pay financial entitlements if they are contractually agreed upon and remain unpaid.
Party_Details.Application: Kea Resources contested the validity of Gan's claims for financial entitlements. Gan stated that he was owed these entitlements as per his employment contract.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court allowed Gan's counterclaim for financial entitlements, awarding him S$42,166.67 and CPF contributions.
